subject: Chemistry
title: The Haber Process Feeds the World
description: One-minute skill: The Haber Process Feeds the World.

hook.text: Half the people alive today exist because of one chemical reaction invented in 1909.

buildup.text: Plants need nitrogen to grow, but atmospheric nitrogen (N₂) is almost inert. Crops were running out of fertilizer.

discovery.text: Fritz Haber forced nitrogen and hydrogen to combine under high pressure and heat, producing ammonia.

twist.text: Haber also developed chemical weapons used in WWI. His wife, a chemist, shot herself in protest.

climax.text: The Haber process now produces 150 million tons of ammonia yearly. It feeds 4 billion people.

punchline.text: One reaction feeds half the world. Its inventor also made poison gas.

quiz.question: What does the Haber process produce?
